comment_text.sn16.1:1.2: This is of course Mahākassapa, “Kassapa the Great”. This chapter paints an indelible personal picture of this great disciple, although I suspect he would have scorned the fame he achieved.
comment_text.sn16.1:1.3: “Improper solicitation” such as hinting, flattery, cosying up to the wealthy, etc.

root_text.sn16.1:1.2: “Santuṭṭhāyaṁ, bhikkhave, kassapo itarītarena cīvarena, itarītaracīvarasantuṭṭhiyā ca vaṇṇavādī;
root_text.sn16.1:1.3: na ca cīvarahetu anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjati; aladdhā ca cīvaraṁ na paritassati; laddhā ca cīvaraṁ agadhito amucchito anajjhāpanno ādīnavadassāvī nissaraṇapañño paribhuñjati.
root_text.sn16.1:2.1: Santuṭṭhāyaṁ, bhikkhave, kassapo itarītarena piṇḍapātena, itarītarapiṇḍapātasantuṭṭhiyā ca vaṇṇavādī; na ca piṇḍapātahetu anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjati; aladdhā ca piṇḍapātaṁ na paritassati; laddhā ca piṇḍapātaṁ agadhito amucchito anajjhāpanno ādīnavadassāvī nissaraṇapañño paribhuñjati.
root_text.sn16.1:3.1: Santuṭṭhāyaṁ, bhikkhave, kassapo itarītarena senāsanena, itarītarasenāsanasantuṭṭhiyā ca vaṇṇavādī; na ca senāsanahetu anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjati; aladdhā ca senāsanaṁ na paritassati; laddhā ca senāsanaṁ agadhito amucchito anajjhāpanno ādīnavadassāvī nissaraṇapañño paribhuñjati.
root_text.sn16.1:4.1: Santuṭṭhāyaṁ, bhikkhave, kassapo itarītarena gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārena, itarītaragilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārasantuṭṭhiyā ca vaṇṇavādī; na ca gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārahetu anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjati; aladdhā ca gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ na paritassati; laddhā ca gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ agadhito amucchito anajjhāpanno ādīnavadassāvī nissaraṇapañño paribhuñjati.
root_text.sn16.1:5.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, evaṁ sikkhitabbaṁ: ‘santuṭṭhā bhavissāma itarītarena cīvarena, itarītaracīvarasantuṭṭhiyā ca vaṇṇavādino; na ca cīvarahetu anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjissāma;
root_text.sn16.1:5.2: aladdhā ca cīvaraṁ na ca paritassissāma; laddhā ca cīvaraṁ agadhitā amucchitā anajjhāpannā ādīnavadassāvino nissaraṇapaññā paribhuñjissāma’.
root_text.sn16.1:6.3: santuṭṭhā bhavissāma itarītarena gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārena, itarītaragilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārasantuṭṭhiyā ca vaṇṇavādino; na ca gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārahetu anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjissāma aladdhā ca gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ na paritassissāma; laddhā ca gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ agadhitā amucchitā anajjhāpannā ādīnavadassāvino nissaraṇapaññā paribhuñjissāmā’ti.
root_text.sn16.1:6.5: Kassapena vā hi vo, bhikkhave, ovadissāmi yo vā panassa kassapasadiso, ovaditehi ca pana vo tathattāya paṭipajjitabban”ti.

translation_text.sn16.1:1.2: “Mendicants, Kassapa is content with any kind of robe, and praises such contentment.
translation_text.sn16.1:1.3: He doesn’t employ improper solicitation for the sake of a robe. He doesn’t worry if he doesn’t get a robe. And if he does get a robe, he uses it untied, uninfatuated, unattached, seeing the drawback, and understanding the escape.
translation_text.sn16.1:2.1: Kassapa is content with any kind of almsfood …
translation_text.sn16.1:3.1: Kassapa is content with any kind of lodging …
translation_text.sn16.1:4.1: Kassapa is content with any kind of medicines and supplies for the sick …
translation_text.sn16.1:5.1: So you should train like this: ‘We will be content with any kind of robe, and praise such contentment. We won’t employ improper solicitation for the sake of a robe.
translation_text.sn16.1:5.2: We won’t worry if we don’t get a robe. And if we do get a robe, we’ll use it untied, uninfatuated, unattached, seeing the drawback, and understanding the escape.’
translation_text.sn16.1:5.3: (All should be told in full the same way.)
translation_text.sn16.1:6.1: ‘We will be content with any kind of almsfood …’
translation_text.sn16.1:6.2: ‘We will be content with any kind of lodging …’
translation_text.sn16.1:6.3: ‘We will be content with any kind of medicines and supplies for the sick …’
translation_text.sn16.1:6.5: I will exhort you with the example of Kassapa or someone like him. You should practice accordingly.”

variant_text.sn16.1:6.5: ovadissāmi yo vā panassa → yo vā pana (bj); yo vā (pts1ed, pts2ed)
